title: Audit position card backed-team / held-side mismatch on live dashboard

context.operator_report: Dashboard sometimes shows that the bot is backing one team when it is really backing the other team.
known_history: This system has had prior BUY_YES / BUY_NO and held-side semantics incidents
Live card baseball semantics were patched before, but the operator is reporting the regression again
context.problem_statement: Need to determine whether the current mismatch is in execution-side truth, dashboard_server payload mapping, or dashboard.html card labeling.

objective: Trace one or more current live positions from execution truth to dashboard card semantics and identify where the side/backed-team mismatch is introduced.
questions: 1. For at least one current live trade, what is the true held contract side from execution/runtime truth?
2. What team should the dashboard describe as backed or faded for that trade?
3. What team/side is the dashboard payload currently presenting?
4. Does the mismatch come from dashboard_server.py payload mapping, dashboard.html labeling logic, or execution truth itself?
5. Is this a regression of a previously fixed semantics bug or a new mapping bug?
acceptance_criteria: At least one current live trade traced end-to-end
Explicit statement of true held side vs displayed side
Exact mismatch point identified or ruled out
Clear recommendation for the next fix task if a bug is found
